str:translations.str:about_feeds: This is a web feed, also known as an Atom feed. Subscribe by copying the URL from the address bar into your newsreader. Visit About Feeds to learn more and get started. It's free.

feed.title: ~/nbaig-dev - virtual-workshops
feed.subtitle: I am a backend web developer with 14+ years of experience. In this blog I will be posting my journey with the Rust Programming Language.

entry.title: Cultivating a Supportive Tech Community Online
entry.summary: In today’s digital age, where technology has become an integral part of our daily lives, online tech communities have emerged as a hub for developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts to connect, learn, and collaborate. However, creating a supportive tech community online isn't always easy. In this blog post, we explore ten tips for building strong connections and fostering a collaborative environment within your online tech community. From defining the purpose of your community to promoting networking and professional development, these tips cover everything you need to know to cultivate a thriving and inclusive online tech community. With clear guidelines, regular events, feedback, and critique, you can create an environment that inspires, motivates, and empowers its members to advance in their careers and make an impact in the industry. So, whether you’re just starting or have been running an online tech community for years, this post is a must-read for anyone looking to build a supportive and thriving community of like-minded individuals.
